solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests compile C++ code that calls the masquerading functions and check they're accessible through the USE_ROCM block in CUDAStream.h. Some pass-to-pass tests do text checks (header guards, trailing whitespace) but the critical behavioral tests use compilation. This is genuine behavioral verification.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (e.g., test_getCurrentHIPStreamMasqueradingAsCUDA) extract the USE_ROCM block from CUDAStream.h and attempt to compile code calling the masquerading functions. In the base commit, these functions are NOT in the USE_ROCM block of CUDAStream.h, so compilation will fail. A stub/no-op change would not make g++ compilation succeed for undefined function calls.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction says masquerading functions should be accessible through c10/cuda/CUDAStream.h's USE_ROCM block. The tests verify exactly this by extracting that block and compiling calls to each of the five named functions plus overloads.
